entries.title: Canonical CloudEvent adapter pattern — 7 rules for CDI async adapters
entries.body:
Canonical CloudEvent adapter pattern — 7 rules for CDI async adapters

Seven rules every CloudEvent adapter in the platform must satisfy.
Discovered during casehubio/connectors#20 — two existing adapters (IoT, Qhorus)
had 2 bugs, 1 silent failure path, and 1 pattern inconsistency. All fixed together.

### The pattern

| Rule | Requirement | Failure mode |
|------|-------------|-------------|
| ObjectMapper | Injected — never a private static instance | Static instance isolates serialisation conf

entries.body:

Quarkus startup fails with an unexpected exception. The stack trace originates in a CDI observer, propagates through `Event.fire()`, exits the `@Observes StartupEvent` handler, and Quarkus marks startup as failed. The application never becomes ready.

A startup hook needs to initialise multiple channels and notify external backends via a CDI event:

v

entries.title: CDI StartupEvent @Priority ordering: no-priority observer fires at 2500, after @Priority(10) — not before
entries.body:
CDI StartupEvent @Priority ordering: no-priority observer fires at 2500, after @Priority(10) — not before

## Symptom

A `@Observes StartupEvent` method with no `@Priority` annotation appears to run *before* another observer annotated with `@Priority(10)`. In reality, the opposite is true: the undecorated observer fires at the CDI default priority (2500), which is *much later* than `@Priority(10)`.

The bug manifests as a startup workaround that "seems to work" because the system falls back to a

Native SQL tables (not JPA entities) are missing after `database.generation=drop-and-create` even though `import.sql` exists in `src/test/resources`. The table was expected to be created by the SQL script.

A test uses a named Hibernate ORM persistence unit (e.g. `quarkus`) configured via `quarkus.hibernate-orm.qhorus.datasource=qhorus`. After switching to `drop

entries.title: Quarkus named persistence unit EntityManager injection uses io.quarkus.hibernate.orm.PersistenceUnit — not jakarta.persistence.PersistenceUnit
entries.body:
Quarkus named persistence unit EntityManager injection uses io.quarkus.hibernate.orm.PersistenceUnit — not jakarta.persistence.PersistenceUnit

## What exists but isn't documented

When using multiple Hibernate ORM persistence units in Quarkus, injecting a specific PU's `EntityManager` requires a Quarkus-specific CDI qualifier — **not** the standard JPA annotation.

**Wrong** (standard JPA — doesn't work as a CDI qualifier):
```java
@PersistenceContext(unitName = "qhorus")
EntityManager em;
```

Scan Java class bytecode in a JAR for annotation presence using Python zipfile + constant-pool string search

## Technique

When you need to verify whether a class in a compiled JAR carries a specific
annotation (e.g. `@Alternative`, `@Default`, `@ApplicationScoped`) without
access to source code or a Java decompiler:

```python
import zipfile

jar = '/path/to/library.jar'
with zipfile.ZipFile(jar) as z:
    data = z.read('com/example/MyClass.class')
    if b'Alternative' in data:
        print(

entries.title: In-memory test doubles are not rolled back when @Transactional rolls back — JTA and non-JTA writes diverge
entries.body:
In-memory test doubles are not rolled back when @Transactional rolls back — JTA and non-JTA writes diverge

## Symptom

A `@Transactional` method writes to both a JPA store and an in-memory `@Alternative` store. A downstream call in the same transaction throws an exception. After rollback:

- The JPA store is clean (the row was rolled back) ✅
- The in-memory store still shows the write ❌

Tests that assert on the in-memory store state after a simulated failure see stale data and pass or fail inc

entries.title: Annotate all 7 CaseMemoryStore public methods with @Timed
entries.body:
Annotate all 7 CaseMemoryStore public methods with @Timed

Every `CaseMemoryStore` adapter that overrides any of the 7 public interface methods must annotate each override with `@Timed(value = "casehub.memory.<adapter>", histogram = true, extraTags = {"operation", "<op-name>"})` where `<adapter>` is the short adapter name (e.g. `jpa`, `sqlite`, `mem0`, `graphiti`) and `<op-name>` matches the method name (e.g. `store`, `storeAll`, `query`, `erase`, `eraseById`, `eraseEntity`, `eraseEntityAcrossTe

IntelliJ MultiHostInjector: multiple addPlace() in one session concatenates content

### Symptom
A string attribute containing `"A${i}x${i}"` produces a single injected file containing `"ii"` instead of two separate files each containing `"i"`. Annotators produce false-positive warnings for the concatenated token (`ii` is unknown; `i` would be known).

### Context
Any `MultiHostInjector` that calls `addPlace()` multiple times within one `startInjecting()`/`doneInjecting()` session for independen

entries.title: CircuitBreakerOpenException escapes AgentInvocationException wrapper in Quarkus langchain4j agents
entries.body:
CircuitBreakerOpenException escapes AgentInvocationException wrapper in Quarkus langchain4j agents

When `@CircuitBreaker` is applied to a Quarkus langchain4j agent interface,
`CircuitBreakerOpenException` is NOT wrapped in `AgentInvocationException`. The FaultTolerance
interceptor fires at a higher CDI priority than the agent's invocation handler, so
`CircuitBreakerOpenException` escapes the wrapper and propagates directly to the caller.
All other agent runtime exceptions ARE wrapped — this is

entries.title: Upstream SPI wrapping for config override — intercept without bypassing declarative flow
entries.body:
Upstream SPI wrapping for config override — intercept without bypassing declarative flow

### Symptom

Need to override annotation values at runtime from Quarkus config, but upstream reads them directly with no interception point.

### Context

Upstream's declarative flow reads annotation values and passes them to builders. The `AgentConfigurator` callback only covers inner `AgentBuilder`, not workflow builders. Options: (A) bypass the declarative path entirely (reimplementing parsing), (B) anno

translates it to a gRPC `FieldCondition`. Only entries matching the filter participate
in the

entries.title: Expose @ApplicationScoped parsing logic as static package-private methods for zero-overhead unit testing
entries.body:
Expose @ApplicationScoped parsing logic as static package-private methods for zero-overhead unit testing

## Context

An `@ApplicationScoped` service class (e.g. a REST resource or business service) contains both injected CDI dependencies and parsing/transformation logic. The parsing logic needs unit tests, but the class can't be instantiated with `new` because CDI proxies it.

## The technique

Extract the parsing/transformation methods as `static` with package-private visibility. Tests instant

entries.title: @All List<T> in Quarkus/ArC gives testable multi-bean injection without Instance<T>
entries.body:
@All List<T> in Quarkus/ArC gives testable multi-bean injection without Instance<T>

## Context
When building a registry or routing layer that needs all CDI beans of a type
(e.g. routing to the right `Connector` by id), the natural CDI approach is
`Instance<T>`. But `Instance<T>` is a CDI interface with many abstract methods
— difficult to stub in plain JUnit tests without a full container.

## What developers normally do
Inject `Instance<T>`, iterate with `.stream()`, or use `@InjectMock` insid

entries.title: CDI bean ordering non-determinism causes silent registry key collision — symptoms mimic scheduler contamination
entries.body:
CDI bean ordering non-determinism causes silent registry key collision — symptoms mimic scheduler contamination

### Symptom
12 @QuarkusTest tests fail with ConditionTimeoutException — cases start (RUNNING)
but workers never execute and cases never reach COMPLETED. Failures appear
test-ordering-dependent: passing in isolation, failing in the full suite. Symptoms
are indistinguishable from Quartz scheduler contamination or event bus saturation.

### Context
Two CaseHub beans — one Java-defined, o

entries.title: Quarkus CDI does not scan @Alternative beans in third-party test jars without explicit index config
entries.body:
Quarkus CDI does not scan @Alternative beans in third-party test jars without explicit index config

## Symptom

`@Alternative @Priority(1)` beans from a `test`-scoped dependency are not selected by CDI in `@QuarkusTest` runs. The default (non-alternative) beans are used instead. No error is thrown — the wrong beans silently win.

## Context

Occurs when a library ships a `-testing` artifact (e.g. `quarkus-mylib-testing`) containing `@Alternative` in-memory implementations intended for use in co

entries.title: Jackson readValue() silently accepts trailing content unless FAIL_ON_TRAILING_TOKENS is enabled
Canonical CloudEvent adapter pattern — 7 rules for CDI async adapters
entries.body:
With `.whenComplete()`: exception preserved on the stage → `thenCompose` skips its lambda
→ exception surfaces to SmallRye → message nacked → retried or dead-lettered.

Rule 3 applies **only in pure fire-and-forget dispatch** (the `CompletionStage` is not
returned or chained further).

CDI `@ObservesAsync` adapters are always fire-and-forget.

SmallRye `@Incoming` consumers return the stage for ack/nack signalling — they are not.

### Alternatives considered

- **Let exceptions propagate from `@

entries.title: quarkus.http.auth.permission.*.policy=deny on /* blocks ALL requests — @RolesAllowed becomes unreachable dead code
entries.body:
quarkus.http.auth.permission.*.policy=deny on /* blocks ALL requests — @RolesAllowed becomes unreachable dead code

### Symptom
Every endpoint returns 403. Always. Regardless of authentication status, role, or `@RolesAllowed` annotation. The application is completely non-functional.

### Context
When adding OIDC + `@RolesAllowed` to a Quarkus application, a natural instinct is to add a catch-all deny policy so missing annotations fail closed:
```properties
quarkus.http.auth.permission.default.pa

entries.title: @WrapBusinessError on Quarkus MCP Server CDI proxy wraps exceptions into ToolCallException(cause) — must inspect getCause() to distinguish error types
entries.body:
@WrapBusinessError on Quarkus MCP Server CDI proxy wraps exceptions into ToolCallException(cause) — must inspect getCause() to distinguish error types

### What happened

`QhorusMcpTools.sendMessage()` throws `IllegalArgumentException("Channel not found: name")` for unknown channels. In a JAX-RS resource, catching `IllegalArgumentException` directly missed this — the test for 404 returned 409 instead.

entries.title: java.net.http.HttpClient.send() does NOT throw for 4xx/5xx — returns HttpResponse with statusCode()
entries.body:
java.net.http.HttpClient.send() does NOT throw for 4xx/5xx — returns HttpResponse with statusCode()

## Symptom

HTTP GET responses with 4xx or 5xx status codes are processed as if they were successful. No exception is thrown. Error body bytes (HTML error pages, JSON error responses) are silently treated as valid response data.

## Context

Polling arbitrary URLs with `java.net.http.HttpClient` and using the response body as data. The code has a `try/catch (IOException e)` block around the `send
